calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-24-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman, via Zoom, for Cruz Outlaw, Administrator.

The court advises that this matter was set for hearing today for an Order to Show Cause RE the Close of Estate and Termination of Further Proceedings for Lack of Assets. A copy of the Order to Show Cause has been served on the parties entitled to notice. The court notes that no objections have been presented, therefore, the court on its own motion orders the estate closed and terminates further proceedings for lack of assets.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial Hearing
Off Calendar May-10-2022 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-12-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman, via Zoom, for Cruz Outlaw, Administrator.

The court queries Mr. Bridgman regarding why he did not appear at the previous calling of this matter. Mr. Bridgman offers testimony regarding why he did not appear including, but not limited to, the filing of a dismissal in this matter in early February of this year. The court reminds counsel that probate administrations cannot be closed via the filing of a dismissal and advises counsel regarding his options for closing the estate. Counsel advises that the value of the estate is less than the minimum for a probate case. Counsel reports that the debt to the CA Franchise Tax Board has been paid.

After additional discussion, the Order to Appear is taken off calendar. The court shall issue an Order to Show Cause why the court should not close this estate on its own motion pursuant to probate code 13500. The Order to Show Cause is set for May-24-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court shall serve the order on all parties who are entitled to notice. Any filings in response to the Order to Show Cause are to be filed by May-10-2022.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-08-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. The Petition set for hearing today is taken off calendar. An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney James Bridgman to appear on Apr-12-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear and for failure to provide notice and provide the court with a proposed order. A new status hearing on close of estate is also set for that date. A Report of Status is to be filed two-weeks prior to the next calling of this matter.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez Not reported.

Petition For Return Of Property To The Estate Pursuant To California Probate Code ?850 And For An Order Finding Financial And Physical Elder Abuse Pursuant To Welfare And Institutions Code 15610 Et Seq
Off Calendar Oct-05-2021 Continued To Dec-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman, via ZOOM, for Cruz Outlaw, Administrator; Michael Marquez, via ZOOM, for Jose Hernandez and Enna Escalante, Objectors.

The court queries Mr. Marquez regarding why he failed to appear at the previous calling of this matter. Mr. Marquez provides oral testimony and the Order to Appear is taken off calendar by the court. The court then requests an update. Both Mr. Marquez and Mr. Bridgman offer reports and oral testimony including issues with availability and incomplete discovery. Mr. Bridgman notes that Mr. Marquez has filed motions which may further delay settlement discussions. Mr. Bridgman also reports that the parties may have an agreement to use Betty Epstein as mediator. Mr. Marquez reports that he doesn't believe that settlement discussions will be productive without complete discovery. The court reminds Mr. Marquez of the history of this matter and it appears to the court that all facts regarding this matter should already be known to the parties so the preceding shouldn't preclude them from engaging in settlement discussions. The court then informs counsel that it will likely be issuing a Case Management Order to help this matter proceed and reminds counsel that sanctions are possible if discovery deadlines are not met. Mr. Bridgman is to monitor the Court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the Case Management Order and serve it on all parties who are entitled to notice.

The matters on today's calendar are continued to Dec-21-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Return Of Property To The Estate Pursuant To California Probate Code ?850 And For An Order Finding Financial And Physical Elder Abuse Pursuant To Welfare And Institutions Code 15610 Et Seq
Off Calendar Sep-21-2021 Continued To Oct-05-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman appeared by zoom for Cruz Outlaw.

Mr. Bridgman stated that he has not heard from Mr. Marquez, that they did not mediate because he could not get a hold of Mr. Marquez, that he tried to get mediation before Bette Epstein and that he thinks this needs to be set for trial. The Petition for Return of Property to Estate, etc., status on filing of a petition for final distribution, and status of mediation are continued to October 5, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., un Dept. 204. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney Michael Marquez, and to the objectors Jose Hernandez and Enna Escalante to appear on October 5, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why mediation has not yet occurred.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Return Of Property To The Estate Pursuant To California Probate Code ?850 And For An Order Finding Financial And Physical Elder Abuse Pursuant To Welfare And Institutions Code 15610 Et Seq
Off Calendar Jun-15-2021 Continued To Aug-31-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman, via ZOOM, counsel for Cruz Outlaw, Administrator; Michael Marquez, via ZOOM, counsel for Jose Hernandez, via ZOOM, and Enna Escalante, Objectors.

The court begins by querying Mr. Marquez as to whether he has received any replies from Mr. Bridgman regarding mediation. Mr. Marquez confirms that has not received a reply from Mr. Bridgman regarding mediation. Mr. Marquez also reports that he has propounded discovery, but that Mr. Bridgman has not replied. The court reminds Mr. Bridgman that the court order regarding mediation stated that mediation should have been completed by June 4, 2021. Mr. Bridgman reports that Mr. Marquez and he have agreed to a mediator and that he has since sent the mediator an email to confirm availability, but he has yet to receive a reply. The court then queries Mr. Bridgman regarding discovery. Mr. Bridgman reports that it has been difficult as his client is currently homeless. The court reminds Mr. Bridgman that discovery is statutorily driven and directs counsel to work on communicating. The court then turns to the Elder Abuse petition and advises Mr. Bridgman that the petition is not properly presented in this matter and that he should consider filing the petition in the Civil division.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Return of Property to the Estate is continued to August 31, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The request for Finding Financial Elder Abuse is denied without prejudice. The Status on Petition for Final Distribution or Petition for Approval of Status of Administration set for June 29, 2021 is continued to August 31, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status of Mediation is also set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Apr-27-2021 Continued To Jun-29-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Return Of Property To The Estate Pursuant To California Probate Code ?850 And For An Order Finding Financial And Physical Elder Abuse Pursuant To Welfare And Institutions Code 15610 Et Seq
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Continued To Jun-15-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman appeared by zoom for Cruz Outlaw (Petitioner).
Michael Marquez appeared by zoom for and with Jose Hernandez by zoom and for and with Enna Escalante by zoom (Objectors).

The Court stated that it does not see a connection between the 850 petition and the elder abuse petition. Mr. Bridgman stated that his Client contributed to the purchase price of the house, that his Client was living in the house, that she is now homeless, that abuse was going on while his Client was living in the house and raised the issue of participating in mediation. The Court suggested filing an elder abuse petition in the Civil Department and stated that it is not sure how much can be done today. Mr. Marquez addressed the issue of Ms. Escalante not having done a response stating that the code permits one of the two parties to respond. Mr. Marquez also stated that Ms. Outlaw was not 65 years of age when this occurred, that they have not stated what the abuse was, that the elder abuse petition is not appropriate in Probate and that he does not stipulate to pro bono mediation. Mr. Bridgman stated that some discovery has been done and that he does not think it is too early to do mediation. The Court orders the parties to participate in pro bono mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than June 4, 2021. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by June 9, 2021. The Petition for Return of Property to the Estate Pursuant to Probate Code 850, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on June 15, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Order signed in open Court. The Parties are to pursue mediation and develop their cases at the same time. Counsel is to monitor the Court's website to obtain a copy of the mediation order. Mr. Bridgman is to serve a copy of the order on Mr. Marquez.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Feb-26-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Feb-26-2020 Continued To Mar-25-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman for and with Cruz Outlaw (Petitioner).

The Court noted that it hasn't had full participation from Ms. Outlaw in carrying out her fiduciary duties, that an inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution are still needed, that both are overdue, and that it required the Court to issue an order to appear and to suspend Ms. Outlaw as the Executor. Mr. Bridgman stated this estate is going to require an 850 petition, that the assets are not in control of the estate, and that they can't do an inventory and appraisal until the 850 petition is heard. Mr. Bridgman also stated that Ms. Outlaw is now in communication with him and that he is confident that she will remain in contact and get this finished. Ms. Outlaw stated that she is committed to do the work. Mr. Bridgman stated that the assets are silver and precious coins that were in the control of a person other than his Client, that he has subpoenaed the company that purchased the coins and that he believes they were worth well over $150,000.00. Status on close of estate is continued to March 25, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A petition for approval of a report on status of administration must be filed by March 18, 2020. Mr. Bridgman is to prepare an order lifting the suspension. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Continued To Feb-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman for Ms. Outlaw.

Mr. Bridgman stated that he has not heard from his Client for many months, that his Client was homeless at one point, that he has no way to contact her, that he does not know if he can proceed without a Client and that he does not want the petition to be dismissed. The Court stated that the Client has a fiduciary duty to bring this estate to a close. Mr. Bridgman stated that he has had no contact with his Client for well over six months, that he only has a phone number, that the last address he had for her was at a shelter, that she is the only one who will benefit, and that the Decedent had gold and silver coins. Mr. Bridgman also stated that Son used those coins to purchase real property, that the coins were worth well over $150,000.00, that the Wife got title of the property transferred jointly with the Son, that he assumes the Son is at the house and that there was no bonding company. The Court suspends Ms. Outlaw as the Executor of the estate. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Executor to appear on February 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show why she should not be removed as the Executor of the estate. The order to show cause will be sent to the address the Court has for her. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Lelu. Not reported.
